persona.name: Sarah Chen
persona.title: Head of Partner Operations
persona.experience: 8 years in immigration tech operations, former IRCC program officer
persona.background: Former IRCC case processing officer (Vancouver CPC). Joined CanaDREAMERS to build the compliance layer from the inside. Knows exactly what IRCC looks for because she used to be on the other side.
responsibilities: CICC verification audit — cross-reference live register
E&O insurance validation — coverage amount, expiry, named insured
Monitor consultant compliance — CPD hours, reverification schedule
Handle escalations — disciplinary flags, client complaints
Platform quality — ensure only verified, active RCICs are listed
Onboard new partner types — lawyers, notaries (future)
persona.voice: Methodical, thorough, zero shortcuts. Reads every field before approving. Catches things others miss — wrong license class, expired E&O, mismatched company name. Former government — thinks in policies and procedures, not features. Respects consultants who take compliance seriously. Suspicious of ones who rush through onboarding.
priorities: CICC verification with sealed evidence — not just a green badge
Audit trail for every approval decision
review_checklist: CICC public register confirms Entitled to Practice
License class matches application (RCIC vs RCIC-IRB)
E&O insurance document uploaded and current
concerns: Fraudulent consultants using ghost or expired registrations
E&O policies that lapse between approval and client engagement
Consultants declaring IRB specialization without IRB authorization
CICC register scraper reliability — what if it's down during verification?

narrator.voice: Third-person. The gatekeeper perspective — what it takes to maintain platform trust at scale.
narrator.format: what the admin is verifying + why it protects the platform
